drizzle-schema.rule: Drizzle table style mirrors agent-mcp: sqliteTable('provider_…', …), text().primaryKey(), integer({mode:'boolean'}) for capability flags, index(...), .references(...). Every table name is prefixed provider_. Capability booleans must read back as booleans (not 0/1) after reopen.

store-class.rule: Store classes mirror agent-mcp: constructor takes a BetterSQLite3Database, methods are thin Drizzle queries, errors are typed ToolError-style codes. ModelStore.resolveModelId(canonical, platform) throws ToolError MODEL_BINDING_NOT_FOUND when absent.

provider-config.rule: The existing ProviderConfig union (anthropic | openai | lmstudio | claudecli) already generalizes provider config; the adapter implements against it. The existing ToolDefinition ({name, description, inputSchema}) is the custom-tool shape the emitter branches away from for server-side tools. ProviderAdapter + StreamChunk are ADDED to agent-mcp-types additively (defined there, implemented here).

runtime-gap.rule: toAnthropicTools() is the concrete FEAT-007 gap: it maps every tool to {name, description, input_schema} and never emits a {type:…} server-side entry. emitToolsForProvider (runtime-tool-forwarding) is the strategic replacement — emitted standalone here (server-side type-tagged + explicit error for unsupported native), wired into anthropic.ts by agent-mcp-refactor (plan 6). The full client-side execution loop stays out of scope.
